name: Lago de Truchillas Trail
name_original: Lago de Truchillas — ida y vuelta por la Sierra de La Cabrera (PR-LE 2)
description: Hike the moderate 7.2-mile Lago de Truchillas Trail, gaining 1785 feet to a glacial lake in 4 hours and 17 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking around Truchas, New Mexico, offers access to the rugged Sangre de Cristo mountains and the vast Pecos Wilderness. The region is characterized by high-altitude peaks, including the Truchas Peaks, which reach over 13,000 feet, and diverse ecosystems ranging from spruce/fir forests to alpine tundra. Hikers can explore a network of trails leading to scenic landmarks such as Pecos Baldy Lake and Truchas Lake, with terrain varying from gentle forest paths to challenging scrambles. This area provides a range of hiking experiences amidst pristine natural landscapes. Best hiking trails around Truchas The most popular hiking route is Cueva de San Genado from Peñalba de Santiago, a 2.8 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This moderate path features a steady ascent through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lago de Truchillas Trail, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route leads through forested areas and open landscapes, offering views of the lake. Local hikers also love the Pico Teleno from Filiel, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Truchas is defined by high-altitude peaks, extensive wilderness, and diverse trails, including those leading to alpine lakes and through dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate day hikes to difficult multi-day expeditions. The routes in Truchas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 290 hikers have used komoot to explore Truchas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Truchas?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Truchas, offering a variety of experiences from moderate day hikes to challenging multi-day expeditions into the Pecos Wilderness. The komoot community has explored more than 290 routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Truchas?
A: Hikes in Truchas feature diverse terrain, from rugged, rocky paths leading to high-altitude peaks like the Truchas Peaks, to forested sections with spruce/fir and aspen groves, and open alpine tundra. You might encounter class 2 and class 3 scrambling on routes to the summits.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options near Truchas?
A: While many trails in Truchas are challenging, some sections or shorter routes can be suitable for families. For example, parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047057004/cueva-de-san-genado-from-penalba-de-santiago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de San Genado from Peñalba de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; trail offer a moderate path with a steady ascent that might be manageable for active families.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Truchas?
A: The Pecos Wilderness, which surrounds Truchas, generally allows dogs on trails, but they must be kept on a leash. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out, especially concerning wilderness areas and any potential restrictions for wildlife protection.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Truchas?
A: Hikers can discover stunning natural features such as the imposing Truchas Peaks, the serene Pecos Baldy Lake, and Truchas Lake, accessible via the Skyline Trail. The region is also home to the picturesque Quemado Fells waterfall. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Bridge of the Arroyo del Silencio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Truchas area?
A: Yes, there are circular hiking routes available. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336452611/pico-tuerto-from-penalba-de-santiago-circular-via-puerto-de-silla-de-la-yegua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto from Peñalba de Santiago — circular via Puerto de Silla de la Yegua&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging loop through mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Truchas?
A: The best time for hiking in Truchas is typically from late spring through early fall (May to October) when trails are generally free of snow and weather conditions are more favorable. High-altitude areas can experience snow well into summer, and multi-day expeditions require careful planning for rapidly changing mountain weather.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the hiking trails?
A: In the village of Truchas itself, you can visit the early nineteenth-century Nuestra Señora del Rosario church. Nearby, you can also explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4640996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalba de Santiago Village&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3781415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Genadio Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hikes in Truchas?
A: Hiking trails in Truchas range from moderate to difficult. Out of the 17 available routes, 10 are rated as moderate and 6 as difficult, often involving significant elevation gain and rugged terrain suitable for experienced hikers. There is also 1 easy route available.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Truchas?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Truchas, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the challenge of the high-altitude peaks, and the serene beauty of the Pecos Wilderness.

Q: Are there opportunities for multi-day hiking expeditions in Truchas?
A: Yes, the Truchas area is ideal for multi-day expeditions, especially for those aiming to summit the Truchas Peaks. Routes to these peaks are extensive and often require overnight stays, with trailheads like Santa Barbara, Borrego Mesa, and Jack's Creek Campground serving as common starting points.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Truchas?
A: The tundra slopes around the Truchas Peaks are known for bighorn sheep sightings. The Pecos Wilderness also offers opportunities for trout fishing in its pristine waters, and you may encounter other high-altitude wildlife.
tips: Nice wooden bridge that helps us cross the Truchillas River when we walk the Path of the Lake of the same name. A delight for lovers of hiking and nature. The route is well signposted.

The surroundings of the lake are ideal for hiking and nature watching. The route to Lake Truchillas is approximately 7 kilometres long and offers spectacular views of the Eria River valley and the typical vegetation of the area, such as heather and juniper. In addition, it is possible to spot diverse fauna, including deer, wild boar and birds of prey such as the golden eagle.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Lago de Truchillas Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally classified as moderate. While the initial section is a wide, gently ascending track, you'll encounter more challenging parts, including narrow, stony paths and a strenuous uphill climb known as the "Cuesta Millín." It's manageable for most hikers, but good physical condition is recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start on a wide, gentle track alongside the River Lago, passing through forests. As you ascend, the path becomes narrower and stonier, with sections like the "Cuesta Millín" being particularly rocky and steep. You'll also walk on a Roman road section and cross two wooden bridges. The vegetation changes from riverbank flora to low-lying scrubland.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail leads to the stunning Lake Truchillas, a glacial lake nestled in a mountainous cirque. Along the way, you'll cross the Truchillas River Wooden Bridge and experience panoramic views of the valley, the Sierra del Teleno, and the Montes Aquilianos. You'll also observe unique geological features like U-shaped valleys and glacial moraines.

faqs.question: Is the Lago de Truchillas Trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as moderate, the trail can be manageable for families or beginners who are prepared for some physically demanding sections. The total distance is around 11.6 km with about 544 meters of elevation gain. It's important to approach it with care, especially the steeper, stonier parts, and ensure everyone has appropriate gear.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is best enjoyed during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring offers lush greenery and wildflowers, while summer provides warmer weather for enjoying the lake. Autumn brings beautiful colors to the landscape. Winter conditions can be challenging due to snow and ice, requiring specialized gear and experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Lago de Truchillas Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, as it is located within a Natural Monument and an area known for wildlife, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local fauna and for their safety, especially given the presence of wildlife like Iberian wolves.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Lago de Truchillas Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: As of current information, there are typically no specific permits required or entrance fees to hike the Lago de Truchillas Trail within the Natural Monument. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations before your visit, as rules can change.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the village of Truchillas. You can usually find parking available in or near the village, which serves as the main access point for the hike. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in wildlife. You might spot deer, wild boars, otters, and various birds of prey. The region is also home to the elusive Iberian wolf and the Pyrenean desman. Remember to observe from a distance and respect their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Does the Lago de Truchillas Trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the official "Lago de Truchillas" trail and also includes sections of the "Senda del Lago Truchillas" path. While it's a distinct loop, you'll be traversing parts of these established routes.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de La Cabrera is a remote and sparsely populated area. While the village of Truchillas is the starting point, facilities like cafes, pubs, or extensive accommodation options are limited. It's recommended to bring your own food and water and plan any overnight stays in advance in nearby, larger towns.
